title: notholaena capillus
intro: Notholaena capillus, also known as the Hair Fern or Golden Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Pteridaceae. It is native to New Zealand and is known for its unique golden-colored fronds. The medicinal properties of Notholaena capillus include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic, which makes it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote overall well-being, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil for medicinal use.
